replies.author: @robinrichey935
replies.text: I’m still not convinced this company’s ad is the famous automobile manufacturer. They clearly looked to be a clothing/costume manufacturer. We can clearly see the “product” is NOT a vehicle of any nature but models that are modeling clothing. Furthermore, the people within the ad did not appear to be a demographic that bought any type of automobile much less one of prominent and historical heritage. I write Nothing derogatory and I do not judge individuals by appearance, I only suggest these people appear to be forward thinking and like the freedom of Uber or Lift conveyance not some ‘outdated’ lifestyle that traps them into purchasing something with a large carbon footprint that depreciates the first day you acquire it and every succeeding day thereafter. Long live the ammonia based internal combustion engines retrofitted to the 50s and 60s vehicles, the lowest carbon based transport!  Cheers

replies.author: @fd502
replies.text:
I think it’s Dengism. This would be a type of Chinese communism that disguises itself as capitalism in which stakeholders are embedded in companies. See if you think this is plausible the definition goes on: corporations are then allowed to continue to function and make a profit as long as they enforce the will of the state. 
Now this all sounds plausible to me I think Ubisoft recently referred to their own stakeholders in a memo addressing them before the shareholders. 
Now maybe you go well where are the profits they’re allowed to keep and yeah that’s baffling, but I don’t wholesale rejection was part of the plan, but the fact that they continue with the ideology despite the losses is some sort of subversion of capitalism.
Then how about this part it’s what’s supposed to happen to citizens: banking and social media access and transportation are treated as privileges and are to be provided as long as the person shares opinions acceptable to the state. That sounds like very familiar especially in regards to social media and I do think Canada went so far as to shut down banking amid a Covid protest did they not?

author: @talbotd27
text: I’m in college right now working on a business marketing degree. Our professor showed us this ad in class yesterday and asked us what we thought. Keep in mind this is a college campus, young and likely left leaning people. This is the EXACT group of people that Jaguar thought they were winning over with this ad. Not a single person liked it. The majority of the class thought it was ridiculous pandering nonsense. The consensus was that it was a bad ad because it is a car ad that does not feature a single car. And I brought up that this was a miss because, among a million other things that they got wrong, they have all the attention now but not a single car for sale in the world. Why do this commercial now when there isn’t any car ready? This has to be the worst rebrand/ company overhaul in history 😂

@Grobut81 Almost half a century ago I recall taking my lunch break at my summer job between years of college. I sat munching my sandwich in my old chevy parked in an alley behind my employer's business. (Atlanta GA.)
There before me across the way was a gorgeous canary yellow E-type ...pushed up next to a wall after the front passenger-side wheel assembly had been crushed in an accident.  All summer long I would gaze every day at that heavenly vision....and I have NEVER forgotten it.  RIP Jaguar.

text: I remember those beautiful 12 cylinder Jaguars from the early 60’s, those precise little valves and beautifully machined arms. My, how far they have fallen…

replies.author: @Philcopson
replies.text: "I remember those beautiful 12 cylinder Jaguars from the early 60’s, those precise little valves and beautifully machined arms. My, how far they have fallen…"   ???  No, you don't:  Jaguar didn't have V12 engines in the 1960s and Jag engines haven't got any rocker-arms;  they're over-head cam - not push-rod. (And rocker-arms are forged, not machined. Apart from everything - spot-on...)

author: @stevendunkley8284
text: Look - if you want to remodel yourself as a luxury brand beyond selling cars then you are not inclusive but exclusive. The cream of the crop only accessible to those wih the coin to purchase it otherwise you would be brokering deals with Walmart and Target if you stand by your renewed mission statement - everyone is entitled to a piece of your pie right? That is literally what you have just said.They don't and they won't so thats what makes them hypocrites and liars.

author: @infiniLor
text: This one at this time is especially disconcerting:  after these past several years watching so many big companies tank themselves  ... so many people quip "follow the money" but here we consistently see the opposite  ... what is going on behind the scenes by what powers to cause all these legacy names to so odiously turn their backs on their demographics,  their bread and butter?
